page.heading: Applications
page.subheading: QS equipment suited for a wide range of industries

index.heading: Applications
index.lede: QS controllers run across eight different machining categories — from metal milling to spring bending, gluing, and food processing. Each case study is its own engineering challenge.

detailPage.heroLede: CNC milling machines use rotating cutting tools to remove material from a workpiece — machining complex custom parts with high precision and high volume for moulds, mechanical components, and medical devices.
detailPage.workflowLede: The QS controller handles the entire chain from CAD/CAM file import to servo control and cycle monitoring. The four steps below describe the typical data and signal flow of a 3-axis milling machine.

workflow.d: NC files are loaded via USB, Ethernet, or DNC. The controller parses and simulates the toolpath on screen before the real run.
256-block look-ahead, S-curve acceleration, and tool-error compensation. The path is NURBS-smoothed for the best surface finish.
Pulse/encoder signals over 1 ms EtherCAT to the X/Y/Z axes and spindle inverter. The closed loop checks position every cycle.
The HMI shows coordinates, speed, spindle current, and alarms. Cycle data is logged for traceability and OEE.

detailPage.specLede: A typical configuration for a mid-size 3-axis milling machine using the QS Astro 6AH controller — suited to moulds and precision mechanical components.

deployments.name: Cơ khí Tân Bình
deployments.loc: HCMC · 2024 · 12 Astro 6AH 3-axis milling machines for an automotive-component line.

deployments.name: Khuôn mẫu Hà Nam
deployments.loc: Ha Nam · 2025 · A 5-axis system with F10T Touch for high-tech plastic moulds.

deployments.name: Y khoa Đồng Nai
deployments.loc: Dong Nai · 2024 · 6 Astro 10i medical milling machines for titanium implant machining.
